name: ESA Copernicus (fleet)

overview.value: The Sentinel missions are a family of Earth observation satellites developed specifically for the operational needs of the European Union's Copernicus programme. Each Sentinel mission is based on a constellation of satellites to fulfil revisit and coverage requirements, providing robust datasets for Copernicus services. The missions employ radar and multi-spectral imaging instruments for land, ocean and atmospheric monitoring.

notes: Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); fields await sourced fill by the weekly maintenance run. Public mission, included on Florian's instruction for competitive context alongside commercial constellations. Converted to a fleet parent (2026-07-05): Sentinel mission families carry the orbit layers.
